etymology.text:
Etymology tree
Proto-Indo-European *kʷetwóres
Proto-Indo-Iranian *čatwā́ras
Sanskrit चतुर् (catúr)
Proto-Indo-European *h₂eng-
Sanskrit अङ्ग (áṅga)
Sanskrit चतुरङ्ग (caturaṅga)bor.
Middle Persian 𐭰𐭠𐭲𐭫𐭠𐭭𐭢 (cʾtlʾng /⁠čatrang⁠/)
Classical Persian چَتْرَنگ (čatrang)bor.
Arabic شطرنج
Borrowed from Classical Persian چَتْرَنگ (čatrang), from Middle Persian 𐭰𐭠𐭲𐭫𐭠𐭭𐭢 (cʾtlʾng /⁠čatrang⁠/, “chess”), borrowed from Sanskrit चतुरङ्ग (caturaṅga, “comprising elephants, chariots, cavalry, and infantry”), from चतुर् (catúr, “four”) + अङ्ग (áṅga, “limb, member”), literally “four limbs; four members”.

entries.body_html:
شطرنج: الشَّطْرَنْج والشِّطْرَنْج: فارسي معرب، وكسرُ الشين فيه أَجود

ليكون من باب جِرْدَحْل.

entries.lexicon_en: Ibn Sīda al-Mursī, Al-Muḥkam wa-l-Muḥīṭ al-Aʿẓam (d. 1066 CE)
entries.lexicon_ar: المحكم والمحيط الأعظم لابن سيده الأندلسي
entries.body_html:
(ش ط ر ن ج)

الشَّطَرَنْج، فَارسي مُعرب عَن ابْن جني قَالَ: وَكسر الشين فِيهِ أَجود ليَكُون من بَاب جِرْدَحل.

entries.lexicon_en: Arabic-English Lexicon by Edward William Lane (d. 1876)
entries.lexicon_ar: المعجم العربي الإنجليزي لإدوارد وليام لين
entries.body_html:
شطرنج



شِطْرَنْجٌ (O, L, Msb, K) and شَطْرَنْجٌ; (L, Msb;) some say the former; and some, the latter; (Msb;) the latter said to be of established authority, as a dial. var., though disallowed in the K [and in the O]; but the former is the more approved on account of its being conformable with Arabic words, (Msb, TA,) such as جِرْدَحْلٌ, (TA,) which the latter is not; (Msb, TA;) and sometimes it is pronounced with the unpointed س, (O,) this being a dial. var.; (K;) A wellknown game; [namely, chess:] (O, L, K:) a Pers\. word, (TA,) arabicized; (Msb, K, TA;) [said to be] from صَدْ رَنْگ “ a hundred stratagems; ” or from شُدْ رَنْج “ trouble departed,”

meaning that trouble departs from him who plays at it; (TA;) [or from شَاهْ رَنْج “ the royal care or sorrow; ” or from شَشْ رَنْگ “ six species or ranks,” because the pieces are of so many species: (Richardson's Pers\. Arab. and Engl. Dict., Johnson's ed.:)] or [accord. to some] it is from الشَّطَارَةُ, (O, K,) or المُشَاطَرَةُ, referring to the word شطرنج; (TA;) or from التَّسْطِيرُ, (O, K,) referring to the word سطرنج; so says Ibn-Hishám El-Lakhmee: but, as IB and others have said, these derivations are only partial, making the ن and the ج to be augmentative letters, and are manifestly incorrect. (MF, TA.)
